SEARCH
Package org.apache.arrow.datafusion.catalog

Interface TableProvider

public interface TableProvider
A table provider that can be registered with a DataFusion session.

This interface allows Java code to implement custom table providers that DataFusion can query. When a SQL query references a table backed by this provider, DataFusion calls scanWithArgs(org.apache.arrow.datafusion.catalog.Session, org.apache.arrow.datafusion.catalog.ScanArgs) to get an execution plan for reading the data.

Example implementation: 

public class MyTableProvider implements TableProvider {
    private final Schema schema;
    private final List<VectorSchemaRoot> data;

    @Override
    public Schema schema() {
        return schema;
    }

    @Override
    public ExecutionPlan scanWithArgs(Session session, ScanArgs args) {
        return new MyExecutionPlan(schema, data, args.projection(), args.limit());
    }
}
See Also:
  • Rust DataFusion: TableProvider

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    default ExecutionPlan
    deleteFrom(Session session, List<Expr> filters)
    Deletes rows matching the filter predicates from this table.
    default ExecutionPlan
    insertInto(Session session, RecordBatchReader input, InsertOp insertOp)
    Inserts data from the given input into this table.
    ExecutionPlan
    scanWithArgs(Session session, ScanArgs args)
    Creates an execution plan to scan this table.
    org.apache.arrow.vector.types.pojo.Schema
    schema()
    Returns the schema of this table.
    default List<TableProviderFilterPushDown>
    supportsFiltersPushdown(List<Expr> filters)
    Returns the filter pushdown support for each filter expression.
    default TableType
    tableType()
    Returns the type of this table.
    default ExecutionPlan
    update(Session session, List<ColumnAssignment> assignments, List<Expr> filters)
    Updates rows matching the filter predicates in this table.

  • Method Details

    • schema

      org.apache.arrow.vector.types.pojo.Schema schema()
      Returns the schema of this table.

      Example: 

      @Override
public Schema schema() {
    return new Schema(List.of(Field.nullable("id", new ArrowType.Int(32, true))));
}
      Returns:
      The Arrow schema describing the table's columns
      See Also:
      • Rust DataFusion: TableProvider::schema

    • tableType

      default TableType tableType()
      Returns the type of this table.

      Default implementation returns TableType.BASE.

      Example: 

      @Override
public TableType tableType() {
    return TableType.BASE;
}
      Returns:
      The table type
      See Also:
      • Rust DataFusion: TableProvider::table_type

    • scanWithArgs

      ExecutionPlan scanWithArgs(Session session, ScanArgs args)
      Creates an execution plan to scan this table.

      The session parameter provides access to the DataFusion session state, which can be used to create physical expressions from the provided filters via Session.createPhysicalExpr(Schema, List).

      See ScanArgs for detailed documentation on the filter, projection, and limit parameters.

      Example: 

      @Override
public ExecutionPlan scanWithArgs(Session session, ScanArgs args) {
    List<Integer> proj = args.projection();
    Long limit = args.limit();
    return new MyExecutionPlan(schema(), data, proj, limit);
}
      Parameters:
      session - The session context for this scan (borrowed, valid only during this call)
      args - The scan arguments containing filters, projection, and limit
      Returns:
      An execution plan that produces the requested data
      Throws:
      DataFusionError - if creating the scan fails
      See Also:
      • Rust DataFusion: TableProvider::scan_with_args

    • supportsFiltersPushdown

      default List<TableProviderFilterPushDown> supportsFiltersPushdown(List<Expr> filters)
      Returns the filter pushdown support for each filter expression.

      DataFusion calls this method to determine which filters the provider can handle natively. The returned list must have the same size as the input filters list, with one TableProviderFilterPushDown value per filter.

      The default implementation returns TableProviderFilterPushDown.INEXACT for all filters, meaning all filters are passed to scanWithArgs(org.apache.arrow.datafusion.catalog.Session, org.apache.arrow.datafusion.catalog.ScanArgs) but DataFusion will also re-apply them after scan to ensure correctness.

      Example: 

      @Override
public List<TableProviderFilterPushDown> supportsFiltersPushdown(List<Expr> filters) {
    return filters.stream()
        .map(f -> TableProviderFilterPushDown.EXACT)
        .toList();
}
      Parameters:
      filters - the filter expressions to evaluate (borrowed, valid only during this call)
      Returns:
      a list of pushdown support values, one per filter
      See Also:
      • Rust DataFusion: TableProvider::supports_filters_pushdown

    • insertInto

      default ExecutionPlan insertInto(Session session, RecordBatchReader input, InsertOp insertOp)
      Inserts data from the given input into this table.

      The input is a RecordBatchReader that provides the data to insert. The reader is lazily evaluated — each call to RecordBatchReader.loadNextBatch() fetches exactly one batch from the DataFusion execution plan. The caller is responsible for closing the reader.

      Returns an ExecutionPlan that produces a single row with a count column (UInt64) indicating the number of rows inserted.

      The default implementation throws UnsupportedOperationException.

      Example: 

      @Override
public ExecutionPlan insertInto(Session session, RecordBatchReader input, InsertOp insertOp) {
    int count = 0;
    while (input.loadNextBatch()) {
        count += input.getVectorSchemaRoot().getRowCount();
    }
    return new CountResultPlan(schema(), count);
}
      Parameters:
      session - the session context (borrowed, valid only during this call)
      input - the data to insert (borrowed, valid only during this call)
      insertOp - the insert operation type (append, overwrite, or replace)
      Returns:
      an execution plan producing the insert count
      Throws:
      UnsupportedOperationException - if this table does not support inserts
      See Also:
      • Rust DataFusion: TableProvider::insert_into

    • deleteFrom

      default ExecutionPlan deleteFrom(Session session, List<Expr> filters)
      Deletes rows matching the filter predicates from this table.

      Returns an ExecutionPlan that produces a single row with a count column (UInt64) indicating the number of rows deleted. An empty filter list deletes all rows.

      The default implementation throws UnsupportedOperationException.

      Example: 

      @Override
public ExecutionPlan deleteFrom(Session session, List<Expr> filters) {
    int deleted = storage.deleteWhere(filters);
    return new CountResultPlan(schema(), deleted);
}
      Parameters:
      session - the session context (borrowed, valid only during this call)
      filters - the filter predicates for rows to delete (empty means delete all)
      Returns:
      an execution plan producing the delete count
      Throws:
      UnsupportedOperationException - if this table does not support deletes
      See Also:
      • Rust DataFusion: TableProvider::delete_from

    • update

      default ExecutionPlan update(Session session, List<ColumnAssignment> assignments, List<Expr> filters)
      Updates rows matching the filter predicates in this table.

      Returns an ExecutionPlan that produces a single row with a count column (UInt64) indicating the number of rows updated. An empty filter list updates all rows.

      The default implementation throws UnsupportedOperationException.

      Example: 

      @Override
public ExecutionPlan update(Session session, List<ColumnAssignment> assignments,
        List<Expr> filters) {
    int updated = storage.updateWhere(assignments, filters);
    return new CountResultPlan(schema(), updated);
}
      Parameters:
      session - the session context (borrowed, valid only during this call)
      assignments - the column assignments to apply (column name + new value expression)
      filters - the filter predicates for rows to update (empty means update all)
      Returns:
      an execution plan producing the update count
      Throws:
      UnsupportedOperationException - if this table does not support updates
      See Also:
      • Rust DataFusion: TableProvider::update
